theme: reconstructing a lost tax code from payroll scrolls
title: The Notch in the Scrolls — reconstructing a lost bracket from a bunching pile-up
strategies: trivial: flat single-rate guess from hours/wages using only the lower half of the archived wage window -> reproduces the checker's own baseline (~0.1)

greedy: textbook flat single-rate fit from hours/wages using the WHOLE archive; folds the bunched workers in as ordinary data points rather than recognizing a pile-up at a hidden threshold, so it never reconstructs a second bracket and carries no structural information about the unobserved upper bracket

strong: locate the bunching ATOM in the z=wage*hours histogram (not a curve fit) to read off the notch z0; fit tau_lo on the smooth region only, excluding the bunched cluster; the bunched fraction of the archive, combined with the known wage window, directly locates the wage of the marginal buncher; combine with the given surcharge dT to solve a closed-form indifference equation for the unobserved upper-bracket rate tau_hi

headroom above strong: robustify the atom-detection against mis-transcribed (outlier) scroll entries and correct for the small residual bias the through-origin tau_lo fit inherits from noise, pushing held-out error toward the idiosyncratic labor-supply noise floor
